title: Shared CLI core across monorepo packages — commonalities, layering, and scope
question: What are the underlying commonalities any monorepo package CLI needs (pi-context-cli today; future pi-workflows / jit-agent-dispatch CLIs) so we do not duplicate code — a single source for what is best single-source-editable, with each package adding its particulars — from a minimalist best-practices perspective?
method: Code-inspection via parallel Explore agents: mapped pi-context-cli (bin.ts/cli.ts) generic-vs-package-specific machinery, and the op/tool registration shapes + execution models of pi-workflows, pi-agent-dispatch, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents.
scope: src/ tool registration of pi-workflows / pi-agent-dispatch / pi-behavior-monitors / pi-jit-agents
findings_summary:
The duplication risk for monorepo package CLIs is real but narrower than "share OpDefinition."

Corrective finding: OpDefinition is pi-context's INTERNAL wrapper (ops-registry.ts), not a shared contract — registerAll wraps each OpDefinition into pi.registerTool. The contract already single-sourced in-pi is pi-mono's pi.registerTool / AgentToolResult, which all four extensions use identically; there is no in-pi duplication to remove. The risk is entirely in the CLI layer.

What is genuinely common (all generic in pi-context-cli today): argv->params coercion driven purely by the op's typebox parameters schema (--field, @file, scalar/enum/JSON, required-checking); global flags --cwd/--json/--yes/--help; the auth-DECISION logic (authGated + --yes + TTY); help generation; exit codes; and the output layer — OpResult (string|{json}|{read}), renderOpResultText, boundedJsonOutput, and the 50KB cap enforced at the output boundary. That cap was bug-prone this arc (FGAP-013/015: the {json}-channel bypass + the read-.data leak); single-sourcing it stops that class recurring per package. Package-specific is small: the ops registry, a buildContext callback (pi-context's DispatchContext + resolveIdentity + WriterIdentity), the writer-kind vocabulary, and which ops are authGated.

Minimalist shape: one small cli-core package generic over a context type Ctx and a minimal CliOp shape ({name, description, parameters: TSchema, surface, authGated?, run(cwd, params, ctx)}); each package bin ~= runCli({programName, ops, buildContext}). Layering nuance (load-bearing): OpResult/render/cap are used by BOTH the CLI and pi-context's in-pi registerAll (tool text), so they belong in a base both depend on — a tiny op-io package — NOT inside the CLI package (that would invert the extension->CLI dependency). op-io is also where the cap-at-boundary invariant becomes structurally un-bypassable for any surface.

Honest scope limit: a reflection CLI serves only request->response ops. pi-context fits today. pi-workflows fits PARTIALLY (simple ops reflect; workflow-execute/resume are long-running subprocess + checkpoint state + TUI — bespoke, not reflection). pi-agent-dispatch does NOT fit as-is (gating is a pi-runtime pi.on('tool_call') handler). pi-behavior-monitors does NOT fit (tools read runtime state mutated by agent_end handlers). pi-jit-agents is a pure library (no tools, no CLI). So cli-core is the single source for the request->response CLI projection — pi-context now, the request->response subset of pi-workflows later — NOT a uniform four-package CLI. Consistent with the per-package-CLI decision (design-ledger 1c).

Onboarding seam: a package provides an adapter tools->CliOp[]; pi-context's OpDefinition already conforms; pi-workflows wraps its request->response tools' execute->run. The cli-core/op-io split is a PROPOSAL — six open questions (op-io boundary, Ctx-genericity vs a shared identity model, the workflows long-running split, an auth-policy hook, generalizing scripts/parity-check.ts to any cli-core adopter, package count) are recorded in the findings_document; pre-implementation it goes through the canonical pipeline.

dependencies: @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent — pi.registerTool / AgentToolResult is the actual shared in-pi tool contract used identically by all four extensions

stale_conditions: pi-workflows / pi-agent-dispatch / pi-behavior-monitors change their tool registration shape away from the pi-mono pi.registerTool interface.
OpResult / renderOpResultText / boundedJsonOutput / the 50KB boundary cap relocate or change semantics (currently in packages/pi-context/src/ops-registry.ts + read-element.ts).
pi-context-cli's reflection machinery (parseOpArgs / authDecision / the envelope emission) is refactored such that the generic-vs-specific seam shifts.
The per-package-CLI decision (design-ledger 1c) is revisited toward a single meta-CLI.

citations.label: output cap machinery: OpResult / renderOpResultText / boundedJsonOutput / 50KB boundary cap
pi-workflows direct pi.registerTool registration + long-running executors (divergent model)
pi-jit-agents is a library, not an extension (no CLI surface)
